message:
fix(fleet): clamp Rolling batch_size and AllAtOnce parallelism to avoid 0-divisor

- executor.rs: Rolling strategy read batch_size: usize from config and used it
  to advance a `while start < worker_count` loop via `start = end = start +
  batch_size`. If batch_size = 0, start never advances and the fleet CLI
  spins forever. Clamp to .max(1) at the top of the arm, mirroring the
  parallelism.max(1) clamp already used inside deploy_batch.
- dry_run.rs: estimate_total_duration called active.len().div_ceil(parallelism)
  and active.len().div_ceil(batch_size) on raw user-config values, both of
  which panic when the divisor is 0. Same .max(1) clamp.

Both values deserialize from operator-supplied workers.toml / deployment
JSON, so we must not trust them. Tests + clippy green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
